title: Ancient Parrot DNA Points to Pre-Inca Animal Trade
description: Ancient DNA from brightly colored feathers found in Peru shows that at least four distinct species of Amazonian parrots -- the scarlet macaw (Ara macao), the blue-and-yellow macaw (Ara ararauna), the red-and-green macaw (Ara chloropterus) and mealy amazon (Amazona farinosa) -- were transported alive over the Andes centuries before the Inca Empire.

title: Net zero will cost less than fossil fuel price rise, UK climate adviser says
description: Climate Change Committee report comes after days of turbulence in global energy markets

title: Net Zero Costs Less Than One Fossil Fuel Shock, UK’s CCC Says
Analysis of ancient parrot DNA reveals sophisticated, long-distance animal trade network that pre-dates the Inca Empire
description: The sequencing of the ancient parrot DNA has uncovered a thriving network of trade and animal transportation that connected Amazonian forests with arid communities across the Andes.
